source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 4.2 General commands (pp. 31-38)

questions.q: Search the manual page descriptions for every entry mentioning the keyword 'clear'.
questions.why: apropos searches by keyword within the manual pages; 'man -k' is the equivalent option form.

questions.q: Display the one-line description of the 'clear' command only (not a keyword search).
questions.why: whatis prints the short description of the command passed as its argument; 'man -f' is equivalent.

questions.q: Open the manual page for the passwd command.
questions.why: 'man' reads the manual; with no section number it defaults to section 1, telling you about the passwd command and its options.

questions.q: Open the manual for the passwd FILE FORMAT (the /etc/passwd file), i.e. section 5, not the command.
questions.why: Section 5 is 'File formats and conventions'; 'man 5 passwd' documents the file rather than the section-1 command of the same name.

questions.q: If you run 'man passwd' with no section number, which manual section is searched by default?
questions.why: The default section is 1. You can read a section's overview with 'man x intro' where x is the section number.

questions.q: Shut the system down electronically and immediately (force an immediate halt).
questions.why: -h halts (shuts down electronically); 'now' in place of a time forces an immediate stop and sends no message to other users.

questions.q: Reboot the system in five minutes' time.
questions.why: -r restarts; '+mm' schedules the action after that many minutes (an exact clock time would be given as hh:mm).

questions.q: Display the history of commands you have entered.
questions.why: history lists previously entered commands; they are stored in the .bash_history file in your login directory.

questions.q: Delete the command history of the current session (without emptying the .bash_history file).
questions.why: -c clears the current session's history in memory; -w would instead write the current history out to the history file.

questions.q: At the prompt, what does typing '!!' do?
options: Recalls and runs the last command entered
Recalls the command by its number
Recalls the most recent command beginning with '!'
questions.why: !! recalls the last command; !N recalls command number N, and !string recalls the most recent command beginning with that string.

questions.q: Which key completes a command, path or file name, showing the options when pressed twice?
questions.why: Tab completes when there is one solution; pressed a second time it lists the multiple possibilities. No list on a double Tab means there is no completion.
